name: Olstein Law LLC
description_short: Chicago-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, debt collection defense, and consumer protection since 2009.
description_long:
Olstein Law LLC was founded in 2009 and is led by attorney Joseph Olstein, operating from Chicago, IL with service to surrounding areas. The firm is rooted in consumer protection law, with Olstein having worked as an associate attorney at The Law Offices of Chad M. Hayward from 2010-2015 before establishing his own practice. He holds a J.D. from Chicago-Kent College of Law at the Illinois Institute of Technology (2009) and a B.A. in Politics, Philosophy, and Economics from Michigan State University (2005).

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy representation covering Chapter 7 liquidation, Chapter 13 repayment plans, and Chapter 11 reorganization. Beyond bankruptcy filings, they provide debt collection harassment defense and debt settlement services. They also handle real estate law and business bankruptcy matters. The firm accepts clients with and without prior bankruptcy history and structures fees on a case-by-case basis.

Olstein Law LLC distinguishes itself through active professional engagement: Joseph Olstein is a member of the American Bankruptcy Institute and Credit Abuse Resistance Education, and has published in the American Bankruptcy Institute Journal (2016). The firm maintains a consumer-focused mission centered on resolving financial hardship and protecting clients from creditor abuse. They offer free initial consultations with no obligation to hire.

Limitations include the absence of online reviews or client testimonials, which may concern prospective clients evaluating the firm's track record. The firm does not publish pricing information online and requires direct contact for fee quotes. Operating hours are not clearly displayed on the profile, and the firm's web presence is limited to the Lawful directory listing.
pros: 17 years in business (founded 2009) with established track record in bankruptcy law
Attorney published in American Bankruptcy Institute Journal, indicating professional credibility and expertise
Member of American Bankruptcy Institute and Credit Abuse Resistance Education, showing ongoing professional engagement
Handles all major bankruptcy types: Chapter 7, Chapter 13, and Chapter 11 reorganization
Offers debt collection defense and harassment protection, a critical service for financially distressed consumers
Free initial consultations with no obligation to hire, reducing barrier to legal advice
Accepts clients with and without prior bankruptcy history, showing inclusive practice approach
cons: No client reviews or testimonials available on Lawful or other platforms, making it difficult to assess actual client satisfaction
Pricing is not transparent—all services listed as 'contact for price,' requiring direct outreach for cost estimates
Limited online presence beyond Lawful directory; no independent website or detailed service information readily accessible
Operating hours not clearly specified in the profile, creating uncertainty about accessibility
Single attorney firm (Joseph Olstein) may limit availability and capacity during high-demand periods
best_for: Chicago-area consumers filing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy for the first time
Individuals facing active debt collection harassment who need both bankruptcy relief and creditor defense
Small business owners or entrepreneurs considering Chapter 11 reorganization
Consumers with prior bankruptcy history seeking debt settlement or financial restructuring advice
diagnosis: Olstein Law LLC is best suited for Chicago-area consumers or small business owners needing bankruptcy protection, particularly those facing debt collection harassment. The primary caveat is the complete absence of client reviews or online reputation data—prospective clients should specifically request references before retaining the firm, as the Lawful profile itself recommends.
services: Chapter 7 Liquidation bankruptcy filing and representation
Chapter 13 Repayment plan bankruptcy filing and representation
Debt collection defense and harassment protection
Bankruptcy case evaluation for clients with no prior bankruptcy history
Bankruptcy case evaluation for clients with prior bankruptcy history
